entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) studied for its response to phosphorus application in deficient soils.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A soil condition characterized by insufficient phosphorus levels, limiting plant growth.

entities.name: SEEDLING ESTABLISHMENT
entities.description: The critical early growth stage of a plant necessary to achieve satisfactory crop stands.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient applied as fertilizer, such as KH2PO4, to improve growth.

entities.name: LOCALIZED PHOSPHORUS APPLICATION
entities.description: The application of minute amounts of phosphorus (125–500 g P/ha) close to the root system at an early seedling stage.

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The total organic matter of the plant used to measure growth response to phosphorus.

entities.name: POT EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Controlled experimental setups used to evaluate the response of pearl millet hybrids to phosphorus placement.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Geographic regions characterized by soils that are frequently deficient in phosphorus.

entities.name: GENOTYPES
entities.description: Different hybrids and inbred lines of pearl millet, such as ICMP 451-P8 and 81B-P6, exhibiting varying responses to phosphorus.
